title: Ctenopteris maidenii

This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Ctenopteris maidenii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ctenopteris maidenii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Ctenopteris maidenii is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This family consists of approximately 1,500 species of ferns. The classification of Ctenopteris maidenii helps scientists understand its evolutionary relationships and ecological role within its native habitat.
